page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture, this instance utilizes the Neoverse-V2 CPU model operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, meaning hyperthreading is disabled with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The memory configuration consists of 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results show strong memory subsystem performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and large-block memory write bandwidth reaching 86.29 GB/sec (top 25%). Single-core performance is also notable, with Geekbench single-core and text-processing scores placing in the top 25%. However, cryptographic operations represent a performance tradeoff, as the Passmark CPU encryption test scored in the bottom 25%. For machine learning tasks, the instance handles small 135M parameter models with average efficiency, but struggles with larger models, as evidenced by a poor prompt processing speed of 15.66 tokens/sec on a 7B parameter model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides a high density of dedicated physical cores and strong memory bandwidth relative to its resource class, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 instances for balanced workloads. The baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps supports steady data transfer rates. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and text processing tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores and high memory throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and strong large-block memory write speeds. Single-core performance is also strong in text processing and Geekbench tests, though cryptographic performance is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This instance is optimized for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and lightweight text processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.2xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 17 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance for balanced workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory performance for balanced, data-driven workloads.

Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM providing 4.0 GB per dedicated physical core
